steps.title: Attach however to the first clause
steps.content: The sentence first says the supernova began to dim, then explains that supernovas are powerful but short-lived. Here however works best as a parenthetical attached to the first clause before a semicolon introduces the second clause.

steps.title: Reject awkward or fused joins
steps.content: Choice A and choice B place however before although, creating a clumsy double contrast at the start of the next clause. Choice C leaves independent clauses joined only by commas. The remaining choice places however after weeks and uses a semicolon before the although-clause. The answer is D.
